description: Integration Server generates alerts for pre-defined events to provide visibility into the system behavior. For example, alerts are raised when Integration Server license is about to expire or a security certificate is about to expire. These alerts are displayed as notifications in Integration Server Administrator based on system or user-defined criteria.

You can create and manage notifiers using the services in the notifier sub-folder. A notifier defines settings to fetch alerts and perform related tasks. For example, you can create a notifier that can be used to fetch alerts for server errors that are critical and send the notification to an administrator.

In Integration Server Administrator, if you select the Disable Alerts and Notifications option on the Server > Quiesce page and the server is in quiesce mode, alerts are not emitted, and running alerts and notifier services throws an exception.

services.name: pub.alert.notifier:create
description: WmPublic. Creates a notifier.

input_parameters.name: name
description: Name of the notifier.

input_parameters.name: enabled
description: - true to enable the notifier.
- false to disable the notifier. This is the default.
If a notifier is disabled, alerts are not fetched for the notifier.

input_parameters.name: consumptionMode
description: - earliest to get notifications for all alerts in the system. This is the default.
- latest to get notifications only for the alerts emitted after the notifier is created.

input_parameters.name: autoMark
description: - true to automatically mark a notification as read after the service specified in the notifier settings is invoked. This is the default.
- false to manually dismiss a notification from Integration Server Administrator, which marks the notification as read.

input_parameters.name: channelIds
description: Identifies the channels for which you want to receive notifications. You can specify either an individual channel identifier or a list of identifiers. For example, channelIds[0] as 2 for "Server Error" or channelIds[1] as 10 for "Threat Protection" or channelIds[0] as 2 and channelIds[1] as 1 for both.

input_parameters.name: intervalInSeconds
description: Time in seconds after which the system task created for the notifier should check for unread alerts.

input_parameters.name: severityCutoff
description: - Critical. This is the default.
- Error
- Information
- Warning

input_parameters.name: notifyInstantly
description: - true to instantly generate a notification for an alert.
- false if an instant notification is not required for an alert. This is the default.
Note: If intervalInSeconds is also specified, the notifyInstantly parameter takes precedence.

input_parameters.name: webSocketEnabled
description: - true to enable a WebSocket connection to send notifications.
- false to disable a WebSocket connection. This is the default.

input_parameters.name: webSocketSender
description: Optional. Sends a message on a connected WebSocket session.

children.name: endPointURL
description: The URL of the remote WebSocket server that Integration Server uses to send notifications.

input_parameters.name: serviceInvokeEnabled
description: - true to invoke a service using the notifier parameters.
- false to disable service invocation for the notifier. This is the default.

input_parameters.name: serviceInvokeSender
description: Optional. Invokes a service for alerts fetched for this notifier.

children.name: nsName
description: The namespace of the service. For example, pub.flow:tracePipeline .

input_parameters.name: smtpEnabled
description: - true to use an SMTP server to send notifications.
- false if you do not want to use an SMTP server to send notifications. This is the default.

input_parameters.name: smtpSender
description: Optional. Details of the SMTP Server and client used to send the notifications.

children.name: to
description: E-mail address of the recipient for a notification. If you specify multiple addresses, separate them with commas.

children.name: from
description: E-mail address of the sender. If you do not specify this value, Integration Server uses the value specified for the mail.smtp.from JVM property. Furthermore, if a value is not specified for that property, Integration Server uses the default value, user@servername, where the user is the operating system user ID, and servername is the host name of Integration Server.

children.name: mailhost
description: Hostname of the SMTP server.

children.name: mailhostPort
description: Optional. The port on which the SMTP server listens to incoming connections.

children.name: auth
description: Optional. Authorization information that the SMTP service submits to the SMTP server.

children.name: user
description: Username that Integration Server uses to send notifications.

children.name: pass
description: Password associated with the user.

children.name: secure
description: Parameters specifying the security protocol and trust store information to perform certificate validation, which Integration Server uses to communicate with the SMTP server port.

children.name: transport​Layer​Security
description: - none, to use a non-secure mode to communicate with the port on the SMTP server. This is the default.

- explicit, to use explicit security when communicating with the port on the SMTP server. With explicit security, Integration Server establishes an un-encrypted connection to the e-mail server, and then switches to the secure mode.
- implicit, to use implicit security to communicate with the port on the SMTP server. With implicit security, Integration Server always establishes an encrypted connection to the e-mail server.

children.name: truststore​Alias
description: Alias for the truststore that contains the list of certificates that Integration Server uses to validate the trust relationship. If you do not specify a truststore alias, the default truststore alias is used.

output_parameters.name: message
description: Indicates whether the notifier is successfully created or not.

usage_notes: If you specify the intervalInSeconds parameter while creating a notifier, Integration Server creates a simple interval task that runs in the background and does the tasks specified in the notifier such as invoking a service or sending a mail, and so on.

Prior to Integration Server version 10.15, the pub.alert.notifier:create service included the smtpSender/secure/useJSSE input parameter which specified whether Integration Server created the outbound connection using the Java Secure Socket Extension (JSSE) library. Beginning with Integration Serverversion 10.15, Integration Server establishes all secure outbound connections with JSSE. If you migrated to Integration Server 10.15 and a migrated service invokes pub.alert.notifier:create, the smtpSender/secure/useJSSE parameter still appears in the pipeline. However, Integration Server ignores the value of the smtpSender/secure/useJSSE parameter, proceeding as if useJSSE were set to yes.

services.name: pub.alert.notifier:delete
description: WmPublic. Deletes the specified notifier.

input_parameters.name: name
description: Name of the notifier that you want to delete.

output_parameters.name: message
description: Indicates whether the specified notifier is deleted or not.

usage_notes: The simple interval task scheduled for a notifier executes once, even after the notifier is deleted or disabled.
Each notifier maintains a separate record of read and unread alerts. These records are cleared when the notifier is deleted. However, the alerts themselves are not deleted.

services.name: pub.alert.notifier:disable
description: WmPublic. Disables the specified notifier.

input_parameters.name: name
description: Name of the notifier that you want to disable.

output_parameters.name: message
description: Indicates whether the specified notifier is disabled or not.

usage_notes: The simple interval task scheduled for a notifier executes once, even after the notifier is deleted or disabled.

services.name: pub.alert.notifier:enable
description: WmPublic. Enables the specified notifier.

input_parameters.name: name
description: Name of the notifier that you want to enable.

output_parameters.name: message
description: Indicates whether the specified notifier is enabled or not.

services.name: pub.alert.notifier:list
description: WmPublic. Lists all the notifiers in the system.

output_parameters.name: notifiers
description: Document List. The list of notifiers in the system. Each notifier has the following keys:

children.name: name
description: Name of the notifier.

children.name: enabled
description: - true indicates that the notifier is enabled.
- false indicates that the notifier is disabled.

children.name: consumption​Mode
description: - earliest indicates that the notifications are generated for all alerts in the system.
- latest indicates that the notifications are generated only for the alerts emitted after the notifier is created.

children.name: autoMark
description: - true indicates that the system automatically processes the notification. For example, a notification is automatically marked as read after the service specified in the notifier settings is invoked.
- false indicates that a user must manually mark the notification as read.

children.name: channelIds
description: List of identifiers of the channels for which you want to receive notifications.

children.name: interval​InSeconds
description: Time in seconds after which the notifier should check for unread alerts.

children.name: severityCutoff
description: - Critical
- Error
- Information
- Warning

children.name: notifyInstantly
description: - true indicates that notifications are generated instantly for an alert.
- false indicates that notifications are not generated instantly for an alert.
Note: If intervalInSeconds is also specified, the notifyInstantly parameter takes precedence.

children.name: webSocket​Enabled
description: - true indicates that a WebSocket connection is enabled to send notifications.
- false indicates that a WebSocket connection is disabled.

children.name: webSocket​Sender
description: Sends a message on a connected WebSocket session.

children.name: endPointURL
description: The URL of the remote WebSocket server that Integration Server uses to send notifications.

children.name: service​InvokeEnabled
description: - true indicates that a service can be invoked using the notifier parameters.
- false indicates that a service invocation is disabled for the notifier.

children.name: service​InvokeSender
description: Invokes a service for alerts fetched for this notifier.

children.name: nsName
description: The namespace of the service. For example, pub.flow:tracePipeline

children.name: smtpEnabled
description: Specifies whether Integration Server uses an SMTP server to send notifications or not.

children.name: smtpSender
description: Details of the SMTP Server and client used to send the notifications.

children.name: to
description: E-mail address of the recipient for a notification. Multiple addresses are separated with commas.

children.name: from
description: E-mail address of the sender. If you do not specify this value, Integration Server uses the value specified for the mail.smtp.from JVM property. Furthermore, if a value is not specified for that property, Integration Server uses the default value, user@servername, where the user is the operating system user ID, and servername is the host name of Integration Server.

children.name: mailhost
description: Hostname of the SMTP server.

children.name: mailhostPort
description: The port on which the SMTP server listens to incoming connections.

children.name: auth
description: Authorization information that the SMTP service submits to the SMTP server.

children.name: user
description: Username that Integration Server uses to send notifications.

children.name: secure
description: Parameters specifying the security protocol and trust store information to perform certificate validation, which Integration Server uses to communicate with the SMTP server port.

children.name: transport​Layer​Security
description: - none, to use a non-secure mode to communicate with the port on the SMTP server. This is the default.

- explicit, to use explicit security when communicating with the port on the SMTP server. With explicit security, Integration Server establishes an un-encrypted connection to the e-mail server, and then switches to the secure mode.
- implicit, to use implicit security to communicate with the port on the SMTP server. With implicit security, Integration Server always establishes an encrypted connection to the e-mail server.

children.name: truststore​Alias
description: Alias for the truststore that contains the list of certificates that Integration Server uses to validate the trust relationship. If you do not specify a truststore alias, the default truststore alias is used.

usage_notes: Prior to Integration Server version 10.15, the pub.alert.notifier:list service included the smtpSender/secure/useJSSE output parameter which specified whether Integration Server created the outbound connection using the Java Secure Socket Extension (JSSE) library. Beginning with Integration Serverversion 10.15, Integration Server establishes all secure outbound connections with JSSE. If you migrated to Integration Server 10.15 and a migrated service invokes pub.alert.notifier:list, the smtpSender/secure/useJSSE parameter still appears in the pipeline. However, Integration Server ignores the value of the smtpSender/secure/useJSSE parameter, proceeding as if smtpSender/secure/useJSSE were set to yes.

services.name: pub.alert.notifier:update
description: WmPublic. Updates the parameters of an existing notifier.

input_parameters.name: name
description: Name of the notifier.

input_parameters.name: enabled
description: - true to enable the notifier.
- false to disable the notifier. This is the default.
If a notifier is disabled, alerts are not fetched for the notifier.

input_parameters.name: consumptionMode
description: - earliest to get notifications for all alerts in the system. This is the default.
- latest to get notifications only for the alerts emitted after the notifier is created.

input_parameters.name: autoMark
description: - true to automatically mark a notification as read after the service specified in the notifier settings is invoked. This is the default.
- false to manually dismiss a notification from Integration Server Administrator, which marks the notification as read.

input_parameters.name: channelIds
description: Identifies the channels for which you want to receive notifications. You can specify either an individual channel identifier or a list of identifiers. For example, channelIds[0] as 2 for "Server Error" or channelIds[1] as 10 for "Threat Protection" or channelIds[0] as 2 and channelIds[1] as 1 for both.

input_parameters.name: intervalInSeconds
description: Time in seconds after which the system task created for the notifier should check for unread alerts.

input_parameters.name: severityCutoff
description: - Critical. This is the default.
- Error
- Information
- Warning

input_parameters.name: notifyInstantly
description: - true to instantly generate a notification for an alert.
- false if an instant notification is not required for an alert. This is the default.
Note: If intervalInSeconds is also specified, the notifyInstantly parameter takes precedence.

input_parameters.name: webSocketEnabled
description: - true to enable a WebSocket connection to send notifications.
- false to disable a WebSocket connection. This is the default.

input_parameters.name: webSocketSender
description: Optional. Sends a message on a connected WebSocket session.

children.name: endPointURL
description: The URL of the remote WebSocket server that Integration Server uses to send notifications.

input_parameters.name: serviceInvokeEnabled
description: - true to invoke a service using the notifier parameters.
- false to disable service invocation for the notifier. This is the default.

input_parameters.name: serviceInvokeSender
description: Optional. Invokes a service for alerts fetched for this notifier.

children.name: nsName
description: The namespace of the service. For example, pub.flow:tracePipeline .

input_parameters.name: smtpEnabled
description: - true to use an SMTP server to send notifications.
- false if you do not want to use an SMTP server to send notifications. This is the default.

input_parameters.name: smtpSender
description: Optional. Details of the SMTP Server and client used to send the notifications.

children.name: to
description: E-mail address of the recipient for a notification. If you specify multiple addresses, separate them with commas.

children.name: from
description: E-mail address of the sender. If you do not specify this value, Integration Server uses the value specified for the mail.smtp.from JVM property. Furthermore, if a value is not specified for that property, Integration Server uses the default value, user@servername, where the user is the operating system user ID, and servername is the host name of Integration Server.

children.name: mailhost
description: Hostname of the SMTP server.

children.name: mailhostPort
description: Optional. The port on which the SMTP server listens to incoming connections.

children.name: auth
description: Optional. Authorization information that the SMTP service submits to the SMTP server.

children.name: user
description: Username that Integration Server uses to send notifications.

children.name: pass
description: Password associated with the user.

children.name: secure
description: Parameters specifying the security protocol and trust store information to perform certificate validation, which Integration Server uses to communicate with the SMTP server port.

children.name: transport​Layer​Security
description: - none, to use a non-secure mode to communicate with the port on the SMTP server. This is the default.

- explicit, to use explicit security when communicating with the port on the SMTP server. With explicit security, Integration Server establishes an un-encrypted connection to the e-mail server, and then switches to the secure mode.
- implicit, to use implicit security to communicate with the port on the SMTP server. With implicit security, Integration Server always establishes an encrypted connection to the e-mail server.

children.name: truststore​Alias
description: Alias for the truststore that contains the list of certificates that Integration Server uses to validate the trust relationship. If you do not specify a truststore alias, the default truststore alias is used.

output_parameters.name: message
description: Indicates whether a notifier is successfully updated or not.

usage_notes: When a notifier is enabled, if consumptionMode is changed from earliest to latest, all alerts generated until then are marked as read.

Prior to Integration Server version 10.15, the pub.alert.notifier:update service included the smtpSender/secure/useJSSE input parameter which specified whether Integration Server created the outbound connection using the Java Secure Socket Extension (JSSE) library. Beginning with Integration Serverversion 10.15, Integration Server establishes all secure outbound connections with JSSE. If you migrated to Integration Server 10.15 and a migrated service invokes pub.alert.notifier:update, the smtpSender/secure/useJSSE parameter still appears in the pipeline. However, Integration Server ignores the value of the smtpSender/secure/useJSSE parameter, proceeding as if useJSSE were set to yes.

services.name: pub.alert:channels
description: WmPublic. Gets all alert channels in the system. Channels help classify alerts and lets you subscribe to a specific type of alerts.

output_parameters.name: Channels
description: All channels have the following keys:

children.name: id
description: Note: Channel IDs for custom channels start with 1001.

children.name: displayName
description: Name of the alert channel.

children.name: description
description: Description for the channel.

children.name: emissionEnabled
description: - true indicates that alerts are emitted for the channel and stored in the database.
- false indicates that alerts are disabled for the channel.

children.name: systemNotificationEnabled
description: - true indicates that notifications are generated for the channel.
- false indicates that notifications are disabled for the channel.

table: Service execution errors. Note: By default, service error notifications are disabled.
Server errors such as HTTP 404. Note: By default, server error notifications are disabled.
A user password is about to expire.
Default administrator password is not changed.
Integration Server license is about to expire.
A security certificate is about to expire.
Multiple login attempt failures that result in a locked account.
Server configuration properties (watt properties) are updated, which requires a server restart.
JDBC or LDAP configuration is changed, which requires a server restart.
Package subscription (Delivery) Package subscription (Release) Note: By default, package management notifications are disabled.
Errors connecting to the Metering agent server.
Default master password is not changed.
IBM webMethods Metering Agent is not configured for on-premises use.
The on-premises Integration Server's fixes are older than fixes on webMethods Integration.

services.name: pub.alert:countAll
description: WmPublic. Gets the count of all alerts in the system, read and unread, or a subset of alerts that meet the filter criteria.

input_parameters.name: filter
description: Optional. To get the count of a subset of alerts, specify the criteria using the following keys:

children.name: startTime
description: Optional. Start of the time interval in which alerts are emitted. Specify the value in UNIX epoch format in milliseconds.

children.name: endTime
description: Optional. End of the time interval in which alerts are emitted. Specify the value in UNIX epoch format in milliseconds.

children.name: severities
description: Optional. List of severities.

children.name: channelIds
description: Optional. List of channel identifiers.

children.name: user
description: Optional. The Integration Server user who triggered the alerts or Administrator for alerts emitted by the server. For example, specify Administrator for Password Management alerts.

children.name: keyword
description: Optional. A keyword that identifies the alerts.

output_parameters.name: count
description: The count of all alerts in the system, read and unread, or a subset of alerts, if the filter criteria are passed as inputs.

usage_notes: To get the count of all alerts in the system, read and unread, do not specify the filter criteria as inputs.

services.name: pub.alert:countUnread
description: WmPublic. Gets the count of all unread alerts in the system or a subset of unread alerts for the notifier and filter criteria.

input_parameters.name: notifierName
description: Optional. Name of the notifier for which alerts are to be fetched. If you do not specify the notifier name, all unread alerts in the system or a subset of the unread alerts that meet the filter criteria are considered.

input_parameters.name: filter
description: Optional. To get the count of a subset of unread alerts, specify the criteria using the following keys:

children.name: startTime
description: Optional. Start of the time interval in which alerts are emitted. Specify the value in UNIX epoch format in milliseconds.

children.name: endTime
description: Optional. End of the time interval in which alerts are emitted. Specify the value in UNIX epoch format in milliseconds.

children.name: severities
description: Optional. List of severities.

children.name: channelIds
description: Optional. List of channel identifiers.

children.name: user
description: Optional. The Integration Server user who triggered the alerts or Administrator for alerts emitted by the server. For example, specify Administrator for Password Management alerts.

children.name: keyword
description: Optional. A keyword that identifies the alerts.

output_parameters.name: count
description: The count of all unread alerts in the system or a subset of unread alerts, if the notifier name and the filter criteria are specified.

usage_notes: Integration Server uses the pub.alert:countUnread service to display the count of unread notifications on the Notifications icon in Integration Server Administrator.
When notifierName is specified in the pub.alert:countUnread service, the severities and channelIds parameters under filter are evaluated in the following manner:

- When only notifierName is specified without the severities and channelIds parameters under filter, the notifier's severityCutoff and channelIds parameters are considered to get the count of unread alerts.

- When notifierName is specified along with the severities and channelIds parameters under filter: If none of the channelIds under filter match with the notifier's channelIds, the notifier's channelIds are considered to get the count of unread alerts. The channelIds under filter are ignored. If none of the severities under filter are within the notifier's severityCutoff, the notifier's severityCutoff is considered to get the count of unread alerts. The severities under filter are ignored. Otherwise, the channelIds and severities under filter are considered to get the count of unread alerts.
Consider a notifier with the following settings and the notifierName is passed to the pub.alert:countUnread service.
- channelIds: 1001, 1002
- severityCutoff: Error

table: Is one of the notifier's channelIds.
Result: Unread alerts for channelId: 1001 with severity: Critical are considered.
Is not among the notifier's channelIds.
Result: Unread alerts for channelIds: 1001 and 1002 with severity: Critical are considered. channelId[0]: 1004 is ignored.
Is one of the notifier's channelIds.
Is not within the notifier's severityCutoff.
Result: Unread alerts for channelId: 1001 with severity: Error or Critical are considered. severities[0]: Warning/Information is ignored.
Is not among the notifier's channelIds.
Is not within the notifier's severityCutoff.
Result: Unread alerts for channelIds: 1001 and 1002 with severity: Error or Critical are considered. channelId[0]: 1005 and severities[0]: Warning/Information are ignored.

services.name: pub.alert:deregisterChannel
description: WmPublic. Deregisters a registered custom channel.

input_parameters.name: channelId
description: Identifier of the channel that you want to deregister.

output_parameters.name: deregistered
description: - true indicates that the channel is deregistered.
- false indicates that the channel is not deregistered.

usage_notes: Channel IDs for custom channels are generated by the system and cannot be modified. The first custom channel gets 1001 as the channel ID and subsequent custom channels have consecutive numbers. Once a custom channel is deregistered, the channel ID is no longer available for a new custom channel until Integration Server is restarted.
Deregistering a channel also deletes all the alerts for that channel.

services.name: pub.alert:emit
description: WmPublic. Generates an alert when a pre-configured condition is met. For example, User or Master Password Expiry, License Key Update, Enterprise Gateway Rule Violation, Service Error, and so on. This service is invoked by Integration Server components to raise alerts.

input_parameters.name: channelID
description: Identifier of the channel associated with the alert.

input_parameters.name: severity
description: Severity associated with the alert channel.

input_parameters.name: subject
description: Subject of the alert message.

input_parameters.name: contentType
description: Type of information in the alert. For example, text/plain.

input_parameters.name: content

output_parameters.name: alertId
description: A unique identifier assigned to each alert emitted.

services.name: pub.alert:fetchAll
description: WmPublic. Fetches all alerts in the system, read and unread, or a subset of alerts that meet the filter criteria.

input_parameters.name: filter
description: Optional. To fetch a subset of alerts, specify the criteria using the following keys:

children.name: startTime
description: Optional. Start of the time interval in which alerts are emitted. Specify the value in the UNIX epoch format in milliseconds.

children.name: endTime
description: Optional. End of the time interval in which alerts are emitted. Specify the value in the UNIX epoch format in milliseconds.

children.name: severities
description: Optional. List of severities.

children.name: channelIds
description: Optional. List of channel identifiers.

children.name: user
description: Optional. The Integration Server user who triggered the alerts or Administrator for alerts emitted by the server. For example, specify Administrator for Password Management alerts.

children.name: keyword
description: Optional. A keyword that identifies the alerts.

input_parameters.name: sortOrder
description: Optional. The order in which the fetched alerts are sorted. Select ASC or DESC. The default is DESC.

input_parameters.name: sortBy
description: - channel
- severity
- subject
- timestamp

input_parameters.name: pageSize
description: The number of notifications on a page.

input_parameters.name: pageNumber
description: Note: Page number starts with 0.

output_parameters.name: alerts
description: A list of the alerts retrieved by the service. Each alert has the following details:

output_parameters.name: id
description: Alert identifier.

output_parameters.name: ts
description: Date and time when the alert is generated, in the UNIX epoch time format.

output_parameters.name: serverId
description: IP address of the host where Integration Server is running.

output_parameters.name: memberName
description: Member name of the machine that hosts Integration Server in a cluster.

output_parameters.name: user
description: Integration Server user who triggered the alert.

output_parameters.name: channelId
description: Channel identifier for the alert.

output_parameters.name: channel
description: Channel for the alert.

output_parameters.name: severityId
description: Severity identifier of the alert.

output_parameters.name: severity
description: Severity of the alert.

output_parameters.name: subject
description: Subject of the alert message.

output_parameters.name: contentType
description: Text that identifies the type of content in the alert such as text/plain and text/html.

output_parameters.name: content
description: Content of the alert.

usage_notes: To fetch all read and unread alerts in the system, do not specify the filter criteria as inputs.
The number of notifications on a page is identified by pageSize and the current page is identified by pageNumber.

services.name: pub.alert:fetchUnread
description: WmPublic. Fetches all the unread alerts in the system or a subset of unread alerts for the notifier and filter criteria.

input_parameters.name: notifierName
description: Optional. If you do not specify the notifier name, all the unread alerts in the system or a subset of the unread alerts that meet the filter criteria are fetched.

input_parameters.name: filter
description: Optional. To fetch a subset of alerts, specify the criteria using the following keys:

children.name: startTime
description: Optional. Start of the time interval in which alerts are emitted. Specify the value in the UNIX epoch format in milliseconds.

children.name: endTime
description: Optional. End of the time interval in which alerts are emitted. Specify the value in the UNIX epoch format in milliseconds.

children.name: severities
description: Optional. List of severities.

children.name: channelIds
description: Optional. List of channel identifiers.

children.name: user
description: Optional. The Integration Server user who triggered the alerts or Administrator for alerts emitted by the server. For example, specify Administrator for Password Management alerts.

children.name: keyword
description: Optional. A keyword that identifies the alerts.

input_parameters.name: sortOrder
description: Optional. The order in which the fetched alerts are sorted. Select ASC or DESC. The default is DESC.

input_parameters.name: sortBy
description: - channel
- severity
- subject
- timestamp

input_parameters.name: pageSize
description: The number of notifications on a page, identified by pageNumber

input_parameters.name: pageNumber
description: Note: Page number starts with 0.

output_parameters.name: alerts
description: A list of the alerts fetched by the service. Each alert has the following details:

output_parameters.name: id
description: Alert identifier.

output_parameters.name: ts
description: Date and time when the alert is generated, in the UNIX epoch time format.

output_parameters.name: serverId
description: IP address of the host where Integration Server is running.

output_parameters.name: memberName
description: Member name of the machine that hosts Integration Server in a cluster.

output_parameters.name: user
description: Integration Server user who triggered the alert.

output_parameters.name: channelId
description: Channel identifier for the alert.

output_parameters.name: channel
description: Channel for the alert.

output_parameters.name: severityId
description: Severity identifier of the alert.

output_parameters.name: severity
description: Severity of the alert.

output_parameters.name: subject
description: Subject of the alert message.

output_parameters.name: contentType
description: Text that identifies the type of content in the alert such as text/plain and text/html.

output_parameters.name: content
description: Content of the alert.

usage_notes: Integration Server uses the pub.alert:fetchUnread service to display the unread notifications in Integration Server Administrator.
When notifierName is specified in the pub.alert:fetchUnread service, the severities and channelIds parameters under filter are evaluated in the following manner:
- When only notifierName is specified without the severities and channelIds parameters under filter, the notifier's severityCutoff and channelIds parameters are considered to fetch the unread alerts.

- When notifierName is specified along with the severities and channelIds parameters under filter: If none of the channelIds under filter match with the notifier's channelIds, the notifier's channelIds are considered to fetch the unread alerts. The channelIds under filter are ignored. If none of the severities under filter are within the notifier's severityCutoff, the notifier's severityCutoff is considered to fetch the unread alerts. The severities under filter are ignored. Otherwise, the channelIds and severities under filter are considered to fetch the unread alerts.
Consider a notifier with the following settings and the notifierName is passed to the pub.alert:fetchUnread service.
- channelIds: 1001, 1002
- severityCutoff: Error

table: Is one of the notifier's channelIds.
Result: Unread alerts are fetched for channelId: 1001 with severity: Critical.
Is not among the notifier's channelIds
Result: Unread alerts are fetched for channelIds: 1001 and 1002 with severity: Critical. channelId[0]: 1004 is ignored.
Is one of the notifier's channelIds.
Is not within the notifier's severityCutoff.
Result: Unread alerts are fetched for channelId: 1001 with severity: Error or Critical. severities[0]: Warning/Information is ignored.
Is not among the notifier's channelIds.
Is not within the notifier's severityCutoff.
Result: Unread alerts are fetched for channelIds: 1001 and 1002 with severity: Error or Critical. channelId[0]: 1005 and severities[0]: Warning/Information are ignored.

usage_notes: The number of notifications on a page is identified by pageSize and the current page is identified by pageNumber. If pageSize is 10 and pageNumber is 0, with sortBy and sortOrder having default values, the latest 10 notifications are listed on the first page.

services.name: pub.alert:getChannel
description: WmPublic. Gets the details of a specific channel.

input_parameters.name: channelId
description: Identifier of the channel that you want to retrieve.

output_parameters.name: displayName
description: Name of the channel.

output_parameters.name: description
description: Description for the channel.

output_parameters.name: emissionEnabled
description: - true indicates that alerts are emitted for the channel and stored in the database.
- false indicates that alerts are disabled for the channel.

output_parameters.name: systemNotificationEnabled
description: - true indicates that notifications are generated for the channel.
- false indicates that notifications are disabled for the channel.

services.name: pub.alert:getSettings
description: WmPublic. Gets the settings of the system notifier.

output_parameters.name: enabled​Emission​Channels
description: List of channels for which alerts can be generated and stored in the database.

output_parameters.name: enabledSystem​NotifierChannels
description: List of channels for which alerts are generated and displayed as notifications.

output_parameters.name: system​Notifier​​Severity​Cutoff
description: Notifications are generated only for alerts with severity equal to, or more than this value.

output_parameters.name: retentionPeriod​InDays
description: Determines how long the alerts are stored in the database.

output_parameters.name: certificate​Expiry​​Warning​InDays
description: Number of days before the certificate expiry date, when the alert should be generated.

services.name: pub.alert:markAllAsRead
description: WmPublic. Marks all alert messages as read.

input_parameters.name: notifierName
description: Optional. Name of the notifier for which all the alerts are to be marked as read. If you do not specify the notifier name, alerts of system channels are marked as read.

output_parameters.name: updateCount
description: Updated count of the unread alerts in the system.

usage_notes: Invoke pub.alert:markAllAsRead to dismiss all notifications at once.

services.name: pub.alert:markAsRead
description: WmPublic. Marks the specified message as read.

input_parameters.name: alertId
description: Specifies the alert that should be marked as read.

input_parameters.name: notifierName
description: Optional. Name of the notifier for which for which the alert should be marked as read.

output_parameters.name: updateCount
description: Updated count of the unread alerts in the system.

usage_notes: Integration Server invokes pub.alert:markAsRead to dismiss a specific notification.

services.name: pub.alert:markAsUnread
description: WmPublic. Marks a specific alert as unread.

input_parameters.name: alertId
description: The alert that you want to mark as unread.

input_parameters.name: notifierName
description: Optional. The notifier for which the alert should be marked as unread.

output_parameters.name: updateCount
description: Updated count of the unread alerts in the system.

services.name: pub.alert:registerChannel
description: WmPublic. Registers a custom channel.

input_parameters.name: displayName
description: Name of the channel that you want to register.

input_parameters.name: description
description: Optional. Description of the channel.

input_parameters.name: emissionEnabled
description: - true to emit alerts for the channel and store them in the database.
- false to disable alerts for the channel.
The default value is true for custom channels as well as system channels other than Service Error and Server Error.

input_parameters.name: systemNotificationEnabled
description: - true to generate notifications for the channel.
- false to disable notifications for the channel.
The default value is true for custom channels as well as system channels other than Service Error and Server Error.

output_parameters.name: channelId
description: Note: Channel IDs for custom channels start with 1001.

usage_notes: For custom channels, channel IDs are generated by the system and cannot be modified. The first custom channel gets 1001 as the channel ID and subsequent custom channels have consecutive numbers. Once a custom channel is deregistered, the channel ID is no longer available for a new custom channel until Integration Server is restarted.

services.name: pub.alert:setSettings
description: WmPublic. Updates the settings of the system notifier.

input_parameters.name: enabled​Emission​Channels
description: List of channels for which alerts can be generated and stored in the database.

input_parameters.name: enabledSystem​NotifierChannels
description: List of channels for which alerts are generated and displayed as notifications.

input_parameters.name: systemNotifier​​SeverityCutoff
description: Notifications are generated only for alerts with severity equal to, or more than this value.

input_parameters.name: retentionPeriod​​InDays
description: Determines how long the alerts are stored in the database.

input_parameters.name: certificate​Expiry​​WarningInDays
description: Number of days before the certificate expiry date when the alert should be generated.

output_parameters.name: message
description: Indicates whether the system notifier settings are updated or not.

usage_notes: Based on the value of the retentionPeriodInDays parameter, a purge job clears the alerts stored in the database.

services.name: pub.alert:severities
description: WmPublic. Lists the pre-defined severities that you can associate with an alert channel. The severity associated with an alert lets you assess the relative impact of a business event on the system.

output_parameters.name: Severities
description: The list of default severities available in the system. By default, 4 severities are configured.

children.name: id
description: 0: Critical, 1: Error, 2: Warning, 3: Information

children.name: displayName
description: The severity name.

input_parameters.name: channelId
description: Note: Channel IDs for custom channels start with 1001.

input_parameters.name: description
description: Optional. Description of the channel.

input_parameters.name: emissionEnabled
description: - true to emit alerts for the channel and store them in the database.
- false to disable alerts for the channel.
The default value is true for custom channels as well as system channels other than Service Error and Server Error.

input_parameters.name: systemNotificationEnabled
description: - true to generate notifications for the channel.
- false to disable notifications for the channel.
The default value is true for custom channels as well as system channels other than Service Error and Server Error.

output_parameters.name: message
description: Indicates whether the channel is successfully updated or not.

usage_notes: Invoke the pub.alert:updateChannel service to ignore a notification of a specific type or restore an ignored notification from Integration Server Administrator.
